description: Space/Enter keydown events from child interactive elements (tag input, tag-add button, tag-remove button, autocomplete items) bubble to the div[role='button'] and trigger its onkeydown handler, which calls e.preventDefault() (blocking normal input) and handleRowClick() (accidentally entering edit mode). This is a direct regression from the previous <button> element, which only activated on Space/Enter when the button itself had focus. With the explicit onkeydown handler on the div[role='button'], ALL Space/Enter keydown events from child elements are captured regardless of focus target.

additionalEvidence.description: tag-input is a child of the div[role='button']; its keydown events bubble to the parent handler
tag-remove <button> child has e.stopPropagation() only for click events (line 369), NOT for keydown events
tag-add <button> child has e.stopPropagation() only for click events (line 425), NOT for keydown events

remediationHint: The onkeydown handler should check e.target to avoid capturing events from child interactive elements (input, button, select, textarea). Alternative: use e.currentTarget === e.target check, or add stopPropagation to keydown handlers on child inputs/buttons.
